qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded batch endpoint allows denial-of-service via concurrent execution of arbitrarily many sub-requests
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets the /batch endpoint's unbounded concurrent Promise.all execution, not protectedFields CLP enforcement for dot-notation query or sort keys.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: User enumeration via verification email request endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets distinct responses from POST /verificationEmailRequest, not protectedFields CLP enforcement for dot-notation query or sort keys.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory root cause: Parse Server protectedFields CLP bypass via dot-notation in query WHERE clauses or sort parameters. The qualifying findings cover unrelated code paths and vulnerability mechanisms.
evidence: The advisory describes protectedFields bypass by using dot-notation such as secretObj.apiKey in query or sort keys, requiring root-field extraction before permission checks.
unbounded-batch-dos reports unbounded /batch request arrays mapped into concurrent promises in src/batch.js.
user-enumeration-verification-email reports distinct unauthenticated /verificationEmailRequest responses in src/Routers/UsersRouter.js.
A targeted search of final report artifacts found no protectedFields, dot-notation query/sort, CLP, or binary-oracle finding matching the advisory-described vulnerable code.
